result.stdout:
Usage:
  Verlite.CLI [options] [<sourceDirectory> [<revision>]]

Arguments:
  <sourceDirectory>    Path to the Git repository. [default: .]
  <revision>           The revision to parse and calculate a version for. [default: ]

Options:
  -t, --tag-prefix <tag-prefix>                                         Tags starting with this represent versions. [default: v]
  -d, --default-prerelease-phase <default-prerelease-phase>             The default phase for the prerlease label.
                                                                        For MinVer compatibility, set this to "alpha.0". [default: alpha]
  -m, --min-version <min-version>                                       The minimum RTM version, i.e the destined version. [default: 0.1.0]
  -p, --prerelease-base-height <prerelease-base-height>                 The height for continious deliverable auto heights should begin at.
                                                                        For MinVer compatibility, set this to 0. [default: 1]
  --version-override <version-override>                                 Force the calculated version to be this version. [default: ]
  --verbosity <normal|verbatim|verbose>                                 The level of logging to output. [default: normal]
  -b, --build-metadata <build-metadata>                                 Set the build data to this value. [default: ]
  -s, --show <all|height|json|major|metadata|minor|patch|prerelease>    The version part which should be written to stdout. [default: all]
  --auto-fetch                                                          Automatically fetch commits from a shallow repository until a version tag is encountered. [default: False]
  --enable-lightweight-tags                                             Create a lightweight tag instead of fetching the remote's. [default: False]
  --enable-shadow-repo                                                  Use a shadow repro for shallow clones using filter branches to fetch only commits. [default: False]
  --dirty-version-bump                                                  Any non-ignored modifications to the repo result in a version bump. [default: False]
  -a, --auto-increment <major|minor|none|patch>                         Which version part should be bumped after an RTM release. [default: patch]
  -f, --filter-tags <filter-tags>                                       Specify a command to execute, an exit code of 0 will not filter the tag. [default: ]
  -r, --remote <remote>                                                 The remote endpoint to use when fetching tags and commits. [default: origin]
  --version                                                             Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                        Show help and usage information

Usage:
  Verlite.CLI [options] [<sourceDirectory> [<revision>]]

Arguments:
  <sourceDirectory>    Path to the Git repository. [default: .]
  <revision>           The revision to parse and calculate a version for. [default: ]

  -p, --prerelease-base-height <prerelease-base-height>                 The height for continious deliverable auto heights should begin at.

  --version-override <version-override>                                 Force the calculated version to be this version. [default: ]

  --auto-fetch                                                          Automatically fetch commits from a shallow repository until a version tag is encountered. [default: False]

  --enable-shadow-repo                                                  Use a shadow repro for shallow clones using filter branches to fetch only commits. [default: False]
